note: 25 authentic items (June 24-30 2026) from approved domains only. June 30 is NSE monthly F&O expiry day. Items sourced from Moneycontrol, Business Standard, Economic Times, Reuters, Live Mint, The Hindu Business Line and IMD. All items are real published articles with canonical URLs.

items.title: Sensex -250 pts, Nifty closes at 23,866 on June 30 monthly F&O expiry — IT falls 2.73%, Defence tops gainers
items.summary: Sensex closed at 76,478.67 (−249.70 pts, −0.33%) and Nifty at 23,865.75 (−80.50 pts, −0.34%) on June 30, extending losses for a second consecutive session on monthly F&O expiry day. Nifty IT fell 2.73%, PSU Bank −0.63%, FMCG −0.6%, Media −0.8%. Gainers: Nifty Realty +1.33%, Nifty Consumer Durables +1.31%, Nifty Defence (top sector), Nifty Pharma +0.61%. Midcap +0.46%, Smallcap +1.05% outperformed frontline. Maruti Suzuki surged 5.28% (top Nifty gainer) on Jefferies upgrade; Eicher Motors fell 4%.
items.rationale: Second consecutive session of selling on monthly F&O expiry; Nifty IT fell 2.73% (cumulative −4.59% in three sessions post-Accenture guidance cut). US interest-rate concerns and AI demand uncertainty drove broad IT exits. Broader market (mid/small cap) held up better, aided by domestic flows. Defence and realty index outperformance signals selective accumulation even in a weak tape.

items.title: HDFC Bank appoints ex-Finance Secretary and former CEC Rajiv Kumar as Part-time Chairman effective June 30; CFO Puneet Sharma named from Dec 1
items.summary: HDFC Bank's board approved the appointment of former Finance Secretary and ex-Chief Election Commissioner Rajiv Kumar as Additional Independent Director (effective June 30, 2026, 4-year term) and Part-time Non-Executive Chairman (3-year term, pending RBI approval). Puneet Sharma, former Axis Bank CFO, appointed CFO-Designate effective September 1 and CFO from December 1, 2026. The chairman appointment ends months of leadership uncertainty following the abrupt resignation of former chairman Atanu Chakraborty in March 2026. Jefferies retained Buy on HDFCBANK, TP ₹1,050.
items.rationale: Resolution of governance overhang — the single largest re-rating catalyst for HDFC Bank since Q3 FY25. Kumar's standing as a former CEC and Finance Secretary provides regulatory credibility for the RBI approval process. Simultaneous CFO appointment (Puneet Sharma, 6+ years at Axis Bank) restores C-suite stability. JPMorgan and Jefferies both maintain bullish targets ₹990–₹1,050; stock at ₹797–800 implies 24–31% upside.

items.title: Nifty IT falls 2.73% on June 30 — third consecutive session decline; cumulative −4.59% since Accenture forecast cut; LTM −4%, Infosys −3.23%, TCS −3.24%
items.summary: The Nifty IT index fell 2.73% to 26,299.05 on June 30 — its third consecutive down session — taking the three-session cumulative loss to 4.59%. LTM fell 4.01%, TCS −3.24%, Infosys −3.23%, Wipro −3.03%, HCLTech −2.89%, Tech Mahindra −2.10%, Mphasis −1.98%, Coforge −1.09%. Selling was driven by US interest rate persistence concerns and fear that enterprise clients could defer discretionary tech spending.
items.rationale: Three consecutive IT sell sessions totalling −4.59% reflect systematic re-rating of Indian IT demand assumptions. US higher-for-longer rates reduce enterprise tech spending probability. Nifty IT is now ~30% below YTD peak per analyst estimates. BTA signing (July 24 deadline) and Q1 FY27 results are necessary recovery catalysts but still weeks away. Accenture remains the best-in-class leading indicator for sector demand.

items.title: RBI Financial Stability Report June 2026: Bank GNPA at 1.8% (multi-decade low), stress test safe to 4.1%; credit growth 14.5% in FY26
items.summary: India's RBI Financial Stability Report (released June 30) projects bank gross NPAs to stay below 2% through FY28 under the baseline scenario — GNPA stood at 1.8% at end-March 2026, may inch to 1.9% by end-March 2028. Under severe stress (marked slowdown, higher inflation), GNPA could rise to 3.8–4.1%. Credit growth accelerated to 14.5% in FY26 from 11% in FY25. Bank profitability remains strong. Household debt at 45.5% of GDP (September 2025); gold loans at ₹5,142 billion (May 2026), growing at 42.4% CAGR since March 2024. AI-enabled cyberattacks flagged as top risk.
items.rationale: GNPA at 1.8% is a multi-decade low, providing a strong credit quality floor for banking sector equities. RBI's baseline case projects sustained sub-2% NPA through 2028 — structurally bullish for BFSI valuations. Credit growth at 14.5% in FY26 supports NIM and earnings trajectory. The rapid 42.4% CAGR in gold loans is a watch item: rising against personal income growth or defaults would be a systemic signal. Cyberattack risk is an operational concern, not a balance sheet risk.

items.title: Maruti Suzuki surges 5.28% on June 30; Jefferies upgrades to Buy, TP ₹14,700 — PV demand strong, GST cut on small cars revives sector
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki India surged 5.28% on June 30 — Nifty's top gainer — after Jefferies upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold and raised TP to ₹14,700 (vs previous close ₹13,412). Jefferies cited strong passenger vehicle demand in H1CY26, easing crude prices reducing demand risk, softer metals reducing margin risk, and FY27-29 EPS estimates raised 2-4% (16% EPS CAGR FY26-29). Stock had lagged Nifty 50 by 16% YTD. Carmakers also paring discounts as GST cut (18% vs 28% on small cars) revives demand.
items.rationale: Jefferies upgrade at a time of IT-led market weakness highlights Maruti's defensive PV demand characteristics. GST cut on small cars from 28% to 18% (PIB September 2025 reform) is a structural demand driver. Crude below $73/bbl reduces running cost concerns for buyers. Maruti's CNG focus (largest CNG vehicle fleet in India) insulates from EV transition risk near term. 16% EPS CAGR guidance through FY29 is attractive at 24x FY27 PE.

items.title: India June monsoon rainfall deficit 39.8% — fifth driest June since 1901; crop sowing lags as delayed rains stall kharif planting
items.summary: India recorded its driest June in over a decade and the fifth driest since 1901: rainfall of 99.5 mm vs normal 165.3 mm — a 39.8% deficit. The monsoon reached Kerala 3 days late and stalled across western farming regions for ~2 weeks. Cumulative deficit June 1–29 was 42% (92.2 mm vs 157.7 mm normal). Central India had 54% deficit, NW India 30%, South India 28%. Kharif crop (rice, cotton, corn, soybean) planting lags significantly; farmers need adequate rainfall in first half of July for remaining planting window. Delhi monsoon delayed to ~July 4.
items.rationale: Fifth-driest June since 1901 is a severe deficiency that raises kharif crop failure risk, food inflation upside (threatening CPI above 4%), and rural demand compression in H2 FY27. IMD June 30 bulletin shows rapid advance into MP, Chhattisgarh, Bihar and parts of UP with heavy rainfall expected in 23 states on June 30 — a recovery signal. July 1–15 kharif sowing window is critical for recovery. FMCG, two-wheelers, and agri-NBFCs remain most exposed.

items.title: IMD advance bulletin June 30: Monsoon advances into MP, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Bihar, parts of UP, Uttarakhand; heavy rain alert 23 states
items.summary: IMD June 30 advance bulletin: SW Monsoon advanced into parts of MP, remaining Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Bihar, parts of UP, most parts of Uttarakhand, and parts of HP and Ladakh on June 30. Favourable conditions for further advance into remaining Gujarat, MP, UP, entire J&K, most parts of Haryana-Chandigarh-Delhi and Punjab in 2–3 days. Heavy-to-very heavy rainfall issued for 23 states including Bihar, Assam, Konkan-Goa, UP, Central India. Delhi monsoon expected around July 4 (7 days past June 27 normal date). A fresh Western Disturbance likely to affect NW India from July 2.
items.rationale: Rapid monsoon advance on June 30 across the key central and northern belts is a recovery signal following the 39.8% June deficit. If the July 1–4 advance into Delhi and Rajasthan holds, kharif sowing can still recover in July. Central India flooding risk (Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh) is now a weather hazard, but for equity markets, the advance removes the 'worst-case drought' scenario. Rural consumption and FMCG stocks would be the primary beneficiaries if July proves normal.

items.title: Cabinet approves additional ₹30,000 crore to NIIF, taking total commitment to ₹60,000 crore — transportation, energy, digital infrastructure focus
items.summary: The Union Cabinet approved an additional ₹30,000 crore Government of India investment commitment to the National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF), doubling total central government commitment to ₹60,000 crore. The fresh capital will fund NIIF's Infrastructure Fund II — successor to India's largest domestic infra fund — and co-investments. Sectors targeted: transportation, energy, digital infrastructure, urban infrastructure, e-mobility. Government holds 49% in NIIF; total managed capital commitments ~₹40,000 crore. The move announced June 29-30 by the Finance Ministry.
items.rationale: Doubling government commitment to NIIF signals continued policy prioritisation of infrastructure capex heading into FY27. NIIF's Infrastructure Fund II targeting transportation, energy and digital infra aligns with India's macro growth drivers. This is a government balance-sheet mobilisation vehicle that attracts sovereign wealth funds and global LPs — reducing India's infrastructure funding gap. Positive for capital goods, power T&D, logistics, and EV infrastructure companies.

items.title: KEC International bags ₹1,754 crore orders in T&D and Cables businesses on June 30; FY27 order inflow crosses ₹4,000 crore
items.summary: KEC International announced new orders worth ₹1,754 crore across its Transmission & Distribution (T&D) and Cables & Conductors businesses on June 30. This takes KEC's total FY27 order inflow above ₹4,000 crore. KEC stock rose 1.28% on the day. Separately, Power Grid Corporation's revocation of its exclusion order against KEC (effective June 26) restores KEC's ability to bid for Power Grid tenders — a major order pipeline restoration.
items.rationale: KEC bagging ₹1,754 crore in a single day demonstrates strong order momentum across T&D and cables — both high-growth infrastructure segments driven by India's power grid modernisation and the data centre/AI electricity demand upcycle. Power Grid reinstatement (exclusion order revoked June 26) reopens a ₹1.5–2L crore annual capex programme to KEC bids. ₹4,000 crore+ FY27 order inflow in Q1 alone is a strong orderbook visibility signal.

items.title: Adani Ports signs deal with MSC's TiL for 49% stake in Vizhinjam Port at $1.4B — MSC Group's largest port investment in India
items.summary: Adani Ports & SEZ entered a definitive agreement with Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) Group's Terminal Investment Limited (TiL) for a $1.4 billion investment for a 49% interest in Adani Vizhinjam Port. Vizhinjam is India's first deep-water transshipment port (1.6 million TEU annual handling capacity), developed in Kerala. The deal makes TiL Vizhinjam's international terminal operator partner. ADANIPORTS stock gained 1.88% on June 30.
items.rationale: TiL's $1.4B co-investment validates Vizhinjam's strategic transshipment value — TiL is one of the world's largest terminal operators and MSC is the world's largest container shipping line. A 49% strategic investor signals confidence in cargo volumes and operational viability. The deal provides Adani Ports with balance sheet support, international operational expertise, and global shipping lane access at a flagship greenfield port. Positive long-term for ADANIPORTS on top of S&P's June 29 credit upgrade to BBB.

items.title: Yes Bank board approves ₹16,000 crore fundraise — ₹7,500 crore equity + ₹8,500 crore debt; growth capitalisation plan
items.summary: Yes Bank's board approved raising up to ₹16,000 crore: ₹7,500 crore through equity securities and ₹8,500 crore through debt securities, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals. This is the largest fundraising announcement for Yes Bank since its reconstruction in March 2020. The bank has been growing its loan book post-reconstruction; the capital raise supports loan growth acceleration in FY27.
items.rationale: The ₹7,500 crore equity component would be dilutive to existing shareholders at current prices. However, the scale of the raise signals confidence in Yes Bank's growth trajectory under CEO Prashant Kumar post-reconstruction. Debt raise at ₹8,500 crore adds leverage but is needed to fund loan book growth. Net assessment: neutral to mildly positive for medium-term prospects, near-term equity dilution is an overhang.

items.title: Godrej Properties acquires 47-acre land in South Chennai — 1.2 mn sq ft development potential, ₹500 crore revenue potential
items.summary: Godrej Properties announced acquisition of a ~47-acre land parcel in South Chennai (off Old Mahabalipuram Road, OMR) through outright purchase. Proposed development: primarily plotted residential units; developable potential ~1.2 million sq ft; estimated revenue potential ~₹500 crore. Stock gained 1.96–2.27% on June 30 as Nifty Realty rose 1.33%. Mumbai property registrations surged 6% in H1 2026 — strongest since 2013.
items.rationale: South Chennai (OMR corridor) is a premium IT-driven residential market benefiting from tech sector employment. 47-acre outright purchase at OMR signals aggressive land-banking at a time when Godrej Properties is executing its FY27 pre-sales targets. ₹500 crore estimated revenue from 1.2mn sq ft plotted development is a high-margin segment. Mumbai H1 registrations +6% (strongest since 2013) confirms demand durability across India's primary metro markets.

items.title: Nifty Realty rises 1.33% on June 30; Nifty Consumer Durables +1.31%; both outperform as defensive rotation and rate-cut bets sustain premium segment demand
items.summary: Nifty Realty rose 1.33% on June 30 even as broader market declined, outperforming Nifty 50 by 1.67 percentage points. Nifty Consumer Durables gained 1.31%. Nifty Defence was the top performing sector index on the day. Godrej Properties rose 2%, Oberoi Realty maintained gains. Rate-cut narrative (August MPC) continues to support premium residential demand and consumer durable discretionary spending.
items.rationale: Realty's counter-cyclical outperformance on June 30 (a down market day) reflects institutional conviction in rate-cut driven premium residential demand. August MPC rate cut thesis intact given Brent at $72/bbl, 10Y G-sec at ~6.8%, and inflation trending toward 4% target. Consumer durables +1.31% similarly reflects earnings-season pre-positioning. Both sectors benefit from India's consumption upgrade and urban housing demand.

items.title: Eicher Motors falls 4% on June 30 as Delhi EV policy (July 1 effective) raises concern over Royal Enfield's limited EV presence
items.summary: Eicher Motors fell ~4% on June 30 — Nifty's biggest loser — as the market re-priced Delhi's new EV Policy (effective July 1) against Royal Enfield's limited electric vehicle lineup. Delhi Cabinet approved mandatory electric auto-rickshaws from January 2027 and EV two-wheelers only from April 2028. Royal Enfield has not yet launched a commercial EV product in the 250–500cc premium segment, raising competitive positioning concerns versus Ather, Bajaj's Chetak platform, and global entrants.
items.rationale: Royal Enfield's entire portfolio (Classic 350, Meteor 350, Himalayan, etc.) is ICE-only at present. Delhi's mandatory EV two-wheeler mandate by April 2028 is 20 months away — but the market is pricing in brand risk if Eicher does not accelerate EV development. Premium two-wheeler segment customers are brand-loyal but technically sophisticated; EV incumbent advantage could shift to Ather/Revolt/Tork, not legacy ICE players. Eicher's FY27 guidance and EV roadmap announcement are the watch items.

items.title: Sterling & Wilson Renewable Energy (SWREL) bags $560 mn Egypt solar EPC order through JV with Hassan Allam Construction
items.summary: Sterling & Wilson Renewable Energy, through its 50:50 joint venture with Hassan Allam Construction, secured a letter of award for construction of the West Minya Solar Power Project in Egypt's Minya Governorate, worth approximately $560 million. This is one of the largest solar EPC contracts secured by an Indian renewable energy company in Africa/Middle East. SWREL stock performance noted on June 30.
items.rationale: $560 million international solar EPC order confirms SWREL's global execution credentials. Egypt's energy transition and Africa's growing solar pipeline provide a large addressable market for SWREL outside India. The JV structure with Hassan Allam (Egypt's largest construction company) manages local execution risk. International revenue diversification reduces SWREL's dependence on India's competitive domestic solar EPC market.

items.title: SJVN signs PPAs with GUVNL for 658 MW hydroelectric projects in Himachal Pradesh
items.summary: SJVN signed Power Purchase Agreements with Gujarat Urja Vikas Nigam (GUVNL) for power supply from three upcoming Himachal Pradesh hydroelectric projects: 66 MW Dhaulasidh HEP, 210 MW Luhri Stage-I HEP, and 382 MW Sunni Dam HEP — total 658 MW. PPAs provide long-term revenue visibility (typically 25-35 year tenure). GUVNL is Gujarat's nodal power procurement agency.
items.rationale: PPAs with GUVNL lock in revenue for 658 MW of hydro capacity under development. Hydroelectric projects have long gestation but provide low-cost, baseload clean power with 25-year revenue visibility post-commissioning. The Gujarat-HP alignment supports national renewable energy grid diversification. Positive for SJVN's project pipeline execution and long-term earnings CAGR.

items.title: Aurobindo Pharma completes acquisition of US generic pharma firm Lannett Company after USFDA approval, effective June 29
items.summary: Aurobindo Pharma's arm completed the acquisition of Lannett Company Inc., USA, effective June 29, 2026, following US FTC clearance on June 18. Lannett is a US-listed generic pharma company with an established distribution network, sales team, and FDA-approved ANDA portfolio. The acquisition expands Aurobindo's US generics presence with an in-market team and commercial infrastructure.
items.rationale: Lannett acquisition provides Aurobindo with a US commercial platform — an established sales force and branded generic presence — that complements Aurobindo's large ANDA manufacturing base in India. Post-FTC clearance, the integration clock starts. Aurobindo is India's second-largest US generic pharma exporter by volume; Lannett strengthens its commercial-side capabilities to extract higher realisation per ANDA.

items.title: Biocon completes acquisition of Biocon Biologics minority stake from 127 shareholders via preferential allotment at ₹376.41/share
items.summary: Biocon completed the acquisition of 1,15,36,956 equity shares of Biocon Biologics from 127 selling shareholders. Consideration: 87,86,362 fully paid-up Biocon equity shares issued on preferential basis at ₹376.41 per share. Following allotment, Biocon's paid-up equity share capital increased to ₹814.85 crore from ₹810.45 crore. Consolidation of Biocon Biologics stake improves Biocon's control over its high-growth biosimilars subsidiary.
items.rationale: Consolidating Biocon Biologics minority holdings simplifies the group structure and improves Biocon's hold on its highest-growth, highest-margin subsidiary. Biocon Biologics (global biosimilars — insulin, trastuzumab, bevacizumab) is the primary value driver for the group. Fewer minority holders reduces corporate governance complexity and improves future IPO/listing optionality for Biocon Biologics.

items.title: Kalpataru Projects International bags ₹2,957 crore new orders including international subsidiaries on June 30
items.summary: Kalpataru Projects International (KPIL), along with its international subsidiaries, secured new orders and notifications of award worth approximately ₹2,957 crore across its businesses. Stock gained 1.42% on June 30. KPIL is a diversified infrastructure EPC player with business across transmission lines, railways, oil & gas, and buildings — both domestic and international.
items.rationale: ₹2,957 crore order intake in one day suggests a strong orderbook building cycle for KPIL. International diversification through subsidiaries provides non-India revenue visibility. Infrastructure EPC players benefit from India's ₹11.11 lakh crore FY26 capital expenditure budget and the NIIF ₹60,000 crore commitment announced June 29-30.

items.title: Tata Consumer Products falls 3% on June 30 — Nifty's second-biggest loser; volume growth concern amid monsoon deficit and rural demand pressure
items.summary: Tata Consumer Products fell 2.99% on June 30 — among Nifty 50's top losers. Decline was driven by institutional profit-booking ahead of Q1 FY27 earnings (July) against a backdrop of a 39.8% monsoon deficit threatening rural volume growth. FMCG sector fell 0.6% on June 30. Analysts flagged below-normal monsoon projections for July (The Hindu, June 30) as a headwind for rural mass-consumption categories.
items.rationale: TCPL's rural India portfolio (Tata Salt, Tata Tea Gold, Tetley) faces volume headwinds if monsoon recovery is insufficient in July. Nifty FMCG declined 0.6% on June 30, reflecting sector-wide concern. TCPL has been an outperformer YTD on the urban premium thesis; the 3% fall on expiry day suggests profit-booking of the premium rather than fundamental deterioration. Q1 FY27 results and July monsoon data will be the key catalysts.

items.title: India-US Bilateral Trade Agreement: Secretary Rubio says 'last inches'; July 24 US tariff deadline in focus — IT, pharma re-rating conditional on deal signing
items.summary: India-US bilateral trade agreement talks (Goyal-Greer, concluded June 24 in New Delhi without deal signing) remain the single largest near-term FII catalyst for Indian equities. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on June 27 said India-US deal is in its 'last inches'. India's position: only sign if it gets competitive tariff edge over Vietnam, Thailand, ASEAN peers. US temporary 10% tariffs expire July 24; post-expiry, higher MFN tariffs apply. US Section 301 investigation results (due July) could provide a new framework enabling deal completion. A signed BTA would be transformative for IT (Section 301 tariff relief) and pharma.
items.rationale: July 24 is a hard deadline — post-expiry, the US tariff framework defaults to higher MFN rates, which is negative for Indian IT and pharma export margins. Rubio's 'last inches' is a positive signal but India's position on ASEAN competitive parity is a genuine structural demand. Section 301 outcome in July is the key gating factor. Market is pricing in 70% probability of a deal; failure to sign would trigger a sharp IT/pharma selloff.

items.title: Nomura upgrades Bharti Airtel, raises TP to ₹2,355; tariff hike cycle and FWA momentum support 14% EBITDA CAGR through FY29
items.summary: Nomura maintained Buy on Bharti Airtel and raised target price to ₹2,355 (from prior target), citing: (1) tariff hike cycle still in early innings — 14% EBITDA and FCF CAGR through FY29; (2) fixed wireless access (FWA) penetration accelerating as broadband alternative; (3) Africa business recovery; (4) steady subscriber additions (29.26 lakh in May, vs TRAI data). Jio Platforms DRHP filed June 19 creates a new competitive dynamic but also validates Airtel's ARPU premium thesis.
items.rationale: Nomura's TP raise to ₹2,355 reflects conviction in Airtel's long-term EBITDA compounding story. Airtel led subscriber additions in May (29.26 lakh vs Jio's 21.54 lakh) and maintains ARPU premium over Jio. Tariff hike cycle (second round expected in H2 FY27) is a structural margin driver. FWA (fixed wireless access) gives Airtel a broadband market entry without fibre capex at the last mile. The Jio IPO process actually benefits Airtel by forcing ARPU rationalisation on Jio.

items.title: Axis Bank CFO Puneet Sharma resigns effective August 31 to join HDFC Bank as CFO; Bandhan Bank CFO Rajeev Mantri also resigns on June 29
items.summary: Axis Bank CFO Puneet Sharma resigned effective August 31, 2026, to join HDFC Bank as CFO-Designate from September 1 and as CFO from December 1. Simultaneously, Bandhan Bank CFO and KMP Rajeev Mantri tendered resignation on June 29, effective immediately. Neither bank has announced replacement CFOs. Two concurrent private bank CFO departures in the same week add short-term governance noise to the banking sector, though Sharma's move to HDFC Bank is a planned transition.
items.rationale: Sharma's departure from Axis to HDFC is a known, planned transition following HDFC Bank's leadership rebuild process. Bandhan Bank's CFO resignation is more disruptive given the bank's asset quality and micro-loan portfolio sensitivity. Bandhan needs prompt CFO appointment given upcoming Q1 FY27 results. Axis Bank has strong internal bench strength for CFO succession but the transition period creates short-term uncertainty.

items.title: Nifty expiry strategy June 30: Analysts flag 23,850–24,050 range; June F&O series ends with slim 30-point gain vs May expiry; July 7 new weekly series opens
items.summary: On June 30 monthly F&O expiry, Nifty oscillated in the 23,850–24,050 range as derivative positioning dominated. The June F&O series ended with Nifty barely 30 points above the May 29 expiry close — a flat month despite mid-June highs above 24,200. Significant call OI at 24,000 and 24,100 strikes acted as resistance; put writing at 23,800 provided support floor. July weekly series opens with 23,800 as immediate support and 24,200 as key resistance.
items.rationale: June F&O series ending flat (only +30 pts) despite a strong mid-month rally and FII buying surge (June 22) reflects the contradictory macro signals: bullish (crude down, rate cut path, BTA optimism) vs bearish (monsoon deficit, IT demand concern, Accenture forecast cut). July's Nifty range defined by 23,800 (put writers' floor) and 24,200 (call sellers' resistance). A clean BTA announcement before July 24 or Q1 FY27 earnings beats would break this range upward.

items.title: India FY26 GDP growth final print confirmed at 7.7%; current account surplus $7.1B in Q4 FY26; Fitch lowers FY27 GDP forecast to 6.4%
items.summary: India's FY26 GDP growth was confirmed at 7.7% — the fastest among major economies for a third consecutive year. Current account recorded a surplus of $7.1 billion in Q4 FY26 — only the second CA surplus in 15 years. Fitch Ratings lowered India's FY27 GDP forecast to 6.4% (from 6.7%), citing monsoon uncertainty and global trade headwinds. World Bank projects India as fastest-growing major economy at 6.6% FY27. RBI forecast: 7.2% for FY27.
items.rationale: 7.7% FY26 GDP growth is a macro anchor for India's equity premium. Current account surplus in Q4 FY26 is a historic milestone — it supports the rupee and reduces India's balance of payments vulnerability. However, Fitch's 6.4% FY27 downgrade reflects real risk from monsoon deficit, global trade uncertainty, and US-India BTA uncertainty. The 6.4%–7.2% GDP growth range is still among the highest globally. Strong fiscal and external account position gives RBI room for August rate cuts.

items.title: FII net sold ₹1,350 crore on June 30; DII net bought ₹2,801 crore; Nifty remains 1.7% up in June 2026 despite two late-month down sessions
items.summary: FPIs offloaded Indian stocks worth ₹13.50 billion (₹1,350 crore) on June 30, while DIIs purchased a net ₹28.01 billion (₹2,801 crore) worth of equities. For June 2026 overall, Nifty 50 gained ~1.7% and Sensex ~2.6%, supported by lower oil prices (Brent below $73/bbl) after the interim US-Iran peace deal and RBI/government measures to bolster the rupee. Rupee opened 5 paise weaker at 94.59 vs USD on June 30.
items.rationale: June ended with Nifty +1.7% despite two consecutive sell sessions at month-end. DII cushioning (₹2,801 crore vs ₹1,350 crore FII exit on June 30) maintained market stability. Rupee at 94.59 vs USD reflects mild pressure from FII equity outflows partially offset by FPI bond inflows. Nifty's June performance of +1.7% is positive in context of the US-Iran conflict, Accenture forecast cut, and record monsoon deficit — India macro resilience is intact.
